==Pronunciation== * English: Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau5 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-tori ***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-tori ***Onyomi 1: shinyuu ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinyu (신유) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
<!-- == * Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: かのととり (kanoto-tori) ***Onyomi: しんかのとり (shinka-no-tori) ***Onyomi: しんゆう (shinyū) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): sinyu ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
--> ==Years of Yin Metal Rooster== {{Anchors|Xinyou Year|qq=}} A [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] year that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 60 is a '''Yin Metal Rooster year'''.
Yin Metal Rooster is considered to have revolutionary significance.
In the ancient Chinese texts known as {{ill|Wei Shu|ja|緯書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=}} (banned by Emperor [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Yang of Sui]]), with surviving fragments found in [[:en:Miyoshi Kiyotsura|Miyoshi Kiyoyuki]]’s “{{ill|Kakumei Kanbun|ja|革命勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=}}” (collected in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' vol. 26), it is written that following {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Yin Metal Rooster also embodies the metal element, and Xin represents yin energy, often associated with people becoming cold-hearted. Thus, a Yin Metal Rooster year is considered a year when the [[:en:Mandate of Heaven|Mandate of Heaven]] changes, marking revolutionary shifts in [[:en:Dynasty|dynasties]], known as the '''{{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|辛酉革命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛酉}}'''.
In Japan, era name changes ({{ill|Era name|ja|改元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=}}) during Yin Metal Rooster years were meant to prevent political upheaval. This practice started in Yin Metal Rooster of [[:en:901|901]] ([[:en:Shōtai|Shōtai]] 4), when scholar-official Miyoshi Kiyoyuki proposed changing the era name to '''[[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]'''. Frequently, era names were changed consecutively in both {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Yin Metal Rooster years, a custom that continued until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], though such a precedent does not exist in China.
{{Anchors|Xinyou Revolution|qq=}} During the Meiji era, scholar {{ill|Naka Michiyo|ja|那珂通世|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=}} referenced ''Kakumei Kanbun'' with [[:en:Zheng Xuan|Zheng Xuan]]’s annotation: * “The way of Heaven is not distant; after every three and five, it returns. Six cycles ([[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) form one era, four sixes and two sixes overlap, and three sevens multiply to form 21 cycles, totaling 1,320 years.” Based on this, it was believed that every '''1,260 years''' (21 cycles × 60 years = 1,260, called ''Ichibu'') there would be a major revolution during a Yin Metal Rooster year. Applying this, Naka theorized that the 9th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]’s reign ([[:en:601|601]]) marked such a revolutionary year, suggesting that Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]]’s enthronement occurred 1,260 years earlier, in [[:en:660 BC|660 BC]].
Alternatively, some interpret the annotation as indicating a '''1,320-year cycle''' (60 × 22 = 1,320). In this case, the pivotal year would shift to the year of the [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]], three years after Yin Metal Rooster, considered the '''Jiazi Reform Year''' ({{ill|Jiazi Reform|ja|甲子革令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲子}}). This places the starting point at [[:en:664|664]] AD, the year after the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]], aligning with the legendary founding date of 660 BC.
==Months of Yin Metal Rooster== A '''Yin Metal Rooster month''' occurs in [[:en:August|August]] of years ending in 3 or 8 (corresponding to Heavenly Stems {{ill|Gui|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} and {{ill|Wu|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}). Depending on the calendar system, this could refer to the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} month or the solar term period between [[:en:Bailu (solar term)|White Dew]] and the day before [[:en:Hanlu|Cold Dew]].
A '''Yin Metal Rooster day''' is the 7th day in the {{ill|Hassen|ja|八専|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=}} sequence, excluding intercalary days.

Branch]] [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster (雞)]] also represents yin metal, forming a {{ill|harmonious relationship|ja|比和|1=Wu Xing|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|1=比和|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|lt=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|qq=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|4=el|5=Ου Σιν|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/harmonious ==Pronunciation== * English: Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau5 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-tori ***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-tori ***Onyomi 1: shinyuu ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinyu (신유) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
<!-- == * Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: かのととり (kanoto-tori) ***Onyomi: しんかのとり (shinka-no-tori) ***Onyomi: しんゆう (shinyū) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): sinyu ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
--> ==Years of Yin Metal Rooster== {{Anchors|Xinyou Year|qq=}} A [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] year that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 60 is a '''Yin Metal Rooster year'''.
Yin Metal Rooster is considered to have revolutionary significance.
In the ancient Chinese texts known as {{ill|Wei Shu|ja|緯書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|1=weishu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890026}} (banned by Emperor [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Yang of Sui]]), with surviving fragments found in [[:en:Miyoshi Kiyotsura|Miyoshi Kiyoyuki]]’s “{{ill|Kakumei Kanbun|ja|革命勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|1=kakumei kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663819}}” (collected in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' vol. 26), it is written that following {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Yin Metal Rooster also embodies the metal element, and Xin represents yin energy, often associated with people becoming cold-hearted. Thus, a Yin Metal Rooster year is considered a year when the [[:en:Mandate of Heaven|Mandate of Heaven]] changes, marking revolutionary shifts in [[:en:Dynasty|dynasties]], known as the '''{{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|辛酉革命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛酉|1=Yin Metal Rooster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q904743}}'''.
In Japan, era name changes ({{ill|Era name|ja|改元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}}) during Yin Metal Rooster years were meant to prevent political upheaval. This practice started in Yin Metal Rooster of [[:en:901|901]] ([[:en:Shōtai|Shōtai]] 4), when scholar-official Miyoshi Kiyoyuki proposed changing the era name to '''[[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]'''. Frequently, era names were changed consecutively in both {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Yin Metal Rooster years, a custom that continued until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], though such a precedent does not exist in China.
{{Anchors|Xinyou Revolution|qq=}} During the Meiji era, scholar {{ill|Naka Michiyo|ja|那珂通世|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|1=Naka Michiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642793}} referenced ''Kakumei Kanbun'' with [[:en:Zheng Xuan|Zheng Xuan]]’s annotation: * “The way of Heaven is not distant; after every three and five, it returns. Six cycles ([[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) form one era, four sixes and two sixes overlap, and three sevens multiply to form 21 cycles, totaling 1,320 years.” Based on this, it was believed that every '''1,260 years''' (21 cycles × 60 years = 1,260, called ''Ichibu'') there would be a major revolution during a Yin Metal Rooster year. Applying this, Naka theorized that the 9th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]’s reign ([[:en:601|601]]) marked such a revolutionary year, suggesting that Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]]’s enthronement occurred 1,260 years earlier, in [[:en:660 BC|660 BC]].
Alternatively, some interpret the annotation as indicating a '''1,320-year cycle''' (60 × 22 = 1,320). In this case, the pivotal year would shift to the year of the [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]], three years after Yin Metal Rooster, considered the '''Jiazi Reform Year''' ({{ill|Jiazi Reform|ja|甲子革令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲子|1=Yang Wood Rat|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190890}}). This places the starting point at [[:en:664|664]] AD, the year after the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]], aligning with the legendary founding date of 660 BC.
==Months of Yin Metal Rooster== A '''Yin Metal Rooster month''' occurs in [[:en:August|August]] of years ending in 3 or 8 (corresponding to Heavenly Stems {{ill|Gui|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} and {{ill|Wu|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}). Depending on the calendar system, this could refer to the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} month or the solar term period between [[:en:Bailu (solar term)|White Dew]] and the day before [[:en:Hanlu|Cold Dew]].
A '''Yin Metal Rooster day''' is the 7th day in the {{ill|Hassen|ja|八専|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=}} sequence, excluding intercalary days.

Branch]] [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster (雞)]] also represents yin metal, forming a {{ill|harmonious relationship|ja|比和|1=Wu Xing|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|1=比和|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|lt=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|qq=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|4=el|5=Ου Σιν|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/harmonious relationship|13=User:Immanuelle/Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/harmonious relationship|13=User:Immanuelle/Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218652}}.
==Pronunciation== * English: Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau5 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-tori ***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-tori ***Onyomi 1: shinyuu ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinyu (신유) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
<!-- == * Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: かのととり (kanoto-tori) ***Onyomi: しんかのとり (shinka-no-tori) ***Onyomi: しんゆう (shinyū) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): sinyu ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
--> ==Years of Yin Metal Rooster== {{Anchors|Xinyou Year|qq=}} A [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] year that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 60 is a '''Yin Metal Rooster year'''.
Yin Metal Rooster is considered to have revolutionary significance.
In the ancient Chinese texts known as {{ill|Wei Shu|ja|緯書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|1=weishu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890026}} (banned by Emperor [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Yang of Sui]]), with surviving fragments found in [[:en:Miyoshi Kiyotsura|Miyoshi Kiyoyuki]]’s “{{ill|Kakumei Kanbun|ja|革命勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|1=kakumei kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663819}}” (collected in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' vol. 26), it is written that following {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Yin Metal Rooster also embodies the metal element, and Xin represents yin energy, often associated with people becoming cold-hearted. Thus, a Yin Metal Rooster year is considered a year when the [[:en:Mandate of Heaven|Mandate of Heaven]] changes, marking revolutionary shifts in [[:en:Dynasty|dynasties]], known as the '''{{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|辛酉革命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛酉|1=Yin Metal Rooster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q904743}}'''.
In Japan, era name changes ({{ill|Era name|ja|改元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}}) during Yin Metal Rooster years were meant to prevent political upheaval. This practice started in Yin Metal Rooster of [[:en:901|901]] ([[:en:Shōtai|Shōtai]] 4), when scholar-official Miyoshi Kiyoyuki proposed changing the era name to '''[[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]'''. Frequently, era names were changed consecutively in both {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Yin Metal Rooster years, a custom that continued until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], though such a precedent does not exist in China.
{{Anchors|Xinyou Revolution|qq=}} During the Meiji era, scholar {{ill|Naka Michiyo|ja|那珂通世|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|1=Naka Michiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642793}} referenced ''Kakumei Kanbun'' with [[:en:Zheng Xuan|Zheng Xuan]]’s annotation: * “The way of Heaven is not distant; after every three and five, it returns. Six cycles ([[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) form one era, four sixes and two sixes overlap, and three sevens multiply to form 21 cycles, totaling 1,320 years.” Based on this, it was believed that every '''1,260 years''' (21 cycles × 60 years = 1,260, called ''Ichibu'') there would be a major revolution during a Yin Metal Rooster year. Applying this, Naka theorized that the 9th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]’s reign ([[:en:601|601]]) marked such a revolutionary year, suggesting that Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]]’s enthronement occurred 1,260 years earlier, in [[:en:660 BC|660 BC]].
Alternatively, some interpret the annotation as indicating a '''1,320-year cycle''' (60 × 22 = 1,320). In this case, the pivotal year would shift to the year of the [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]], three years after Yin Metal Rooster, considered the '''Jiazi Reform Year''' ({{ill|Jiazi Reform|ja|甲子革令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲子|1=Yang Wood Rat|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190890}}). This places the starting point at [[:en:664|664]] AD, the year after the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]], aligning with the legendary founding date of 660 BC.
==Months of Yin Metal Rooster== A '''Yin Metal Rooster month''' occurs in [[:en:August|August]] of years ending in 3 or 8 (corresponding to Heavenly Stems {{ill|Gui|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} and {{ill|Wu|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}). Depending on the calendar system, this could refer to the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} month or the solar term period between [[:en:Bailu (solar term)|White Dew]] and the day before [[:en:Hanlu|Cold Dew]].
A '''Yin Metal Rooster day''' is the 7th day in the {{ill|Hassen|ja|八専|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=}} sequence, excluding intercalary days.

philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218652}}.
==Pronunciation== * English: Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau5 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-tori ***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-tori ***Onyomi 1: shinyuu ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinyu (신유) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
<!-- == * Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: かのととり (kanoto-tori) ***Onyomi: しんかのとり (shinka-no-tori) ***Onyomi: しんゆう (shinyū) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): sinyu ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
--> ==Years of Yin Metal Rooster== {{Anchors|Xinyou Year|qq=}} A [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] year that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 60 is a '''Yin Metal Rooster year'''.
Yin Metal Rooster is considered to have revolutionary significance.
In the ancient Chinese texts known as {{ill|Wei Shu|ja|緯書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|1=weishu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890026}} (banned by Emperor [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Yang of Sui]]), with surviving fragments found in [[:en:Miyoshi Kiyotsura|Miyoshi Kiyoyuki]]’s “{{ill|Kakumei Kanbun|ja|革命勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|1=kakumei kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663819}}” (collected in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' vol. 26), it is written that following {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Yin Metal Rooster also embodies the metal element, and Xin represents yin energy, often associated with people becoming cold-hearted. Thus, a Yin Metal Rooster year is considered a year when the [[:en:Mandate of Heaven|Mandate of Heaven]] changes, marking revolutionary shifts in [[:en:Dynasty|dynasties]], known as the '''{{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|辛酉革命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛酉|1=Yin Metal Rooster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q904743}}'''.
In Japan, era name changes ({{ill|Era name|ja|改元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}}) during Yin Metal Rooster years were meant to prevent political upheaval. This practice started in Yin Metal Rooster of [[:en:901|901]] ([[:en:Shōtai|Shōtai]] 4), when scholar-official Miyoshi Kiyoyuki proposed changing the era name to '''[[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]'''. Frequently, era names were changed consecutively in both {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Yin Metal Rooster years, a custom that continued until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], though such a precedent does not exist in China.
{{Anchors|Xinyou Revolution|qq=}} During the Meiji era, scholar {{ill|Naka Michiyo|ja|那珂通世|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|1=Naka Michiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642793}} referenced ''Kakumei Kanbun'' with [[:en:Zheng Xuan|Zheng Xuan]]’s annotation: * “The way of Heaven is not distant; after every three and five, it returns. Six cycles ([[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) form one era, four sixes and two sixes overlap, and three sevens multiply to form 21 cycles, totaling 1,320 years.” Based on this, it was believed that every '''1,260 years''' (21 cycles × 60 years = 1,260, called ''Ichibu'') there would be a major revolution during a Yin Metal Rooster year. Applying this, Naka theorized that the 9th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]’s reign ([[:en:601|601]]) marked such a revolutionary year, suggesting that Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]]’s enthronement occurred 1,260 years earlier, in [[:en:660 BC|660 BC]].
Alternatively, some interpret the annotation as indicating a '''1,320-year cycle''' (60 × 22 = 1,320). In this case, the pivotal year would shift to the year of the [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]], three years after Yin Metal Rooster, considered the '''Jiazi Reform Year''' ({{ill|Jiazi Reform|ja|甲子革令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲子|1=Yang Wood Rat|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190890}}). This places the starting point at [[:en:664|664]] AD, the year after the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]], aligning with the legendary founding date of 660 BC.
==Months of Yin Metal Rooster== A '''Yin Metal Rooster month''' occurs in [[:en:August|August]] of years ending in 3 or 8 (corresponding to Heavenly Stems {{ill|Gui|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} and {{ill|Wu|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}). Depending on the calendar system, this could refer to the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} month or the solar term period between [[:en:Bailu (solar term)|White Dew]] and the day before [[:en:Hanlu|Cold Dew]].
A '''Yin Metal Rooster day''' is the 7th day in the {{ill|Hassen|ja|八専|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=}} sequence, excluding intercalary days.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q904743]] Wikidata name: Yin Metal Rooster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Chinese|l=Yin Metal Rooster|kanji=辛酉|romaji=kanoto tori|onyomi=shinyū|p=xīyǒu|w=hsin-yu|mi=ɕin⁵³ i̯ɤʊ̯²¹|y=san1 jau5|j=san1 jau5|ci=sɐn jɐu|mc=sin jɨu|oc-bs=sin jɯʔ|qq=}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yin Metal Rooster also known as Yin Metal Rooster''' ({{ill|1=Yin Metal Rooster|2=ja|辛酉|ja|1=Yin Metal Rooster|2=ja|辛酉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/辛酉|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1=Yin Metal Rooster|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Metal Rooster|qq=}}) is one of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
It is the 58th combination in the cycle, preceded by {{ill|Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|zh|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|ko|경신|fr|Singe de métal|de|Metall-Affe|en|lt_ko=경신|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and followed by {{ill|Yang Water Dog|ja|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|zh|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|ko|임술|fr|Chien de eau|de|Wasser-Hund|en|lt_ko=임술|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Heavenly Stem]] {{ill|Mizunoto (癸)|cdo|Gŭi (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Gui (tige céleste)|gan|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|hak|Kui (thiên-kôn)|ja|Mizunoto|ko|계 (천간)|vi|Quý (Thiên can)|wuu|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|za|Gveiq (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Kúi (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|zh|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} represents yin {{ill|metal|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/metal|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/metal|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster (雞)]] also represents yin metal, forming a {{ill|harmonious relationship|ja|比和|1=Wu Xing|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|1=比和|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|lt=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|qq=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|4=el|5=Ου Σιν|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/harmonious philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218652}}.
==Pronunciation== * English: Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau5 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-tori ***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-tori ***Onyomi 1: shinyuu ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinyu (신유) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
<!-- == * Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: かのととり (kanoto-tori) ***Onyomi: しんかのとり (shinka-no-tori) ***Onyomi: しんゆう (shinyū) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): sinyu ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
--> ==Years of Yin Metal Rooster== {{Anchors|Xinyou Year|qq=}} A [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] year that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 60 is a '''Yin Metal Rooster year'''.
Yin Metal Rooster is considered to have revolutionary significance.
In the ancient Chinese texts known as {{ill|Wei Shu|ja|緯書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|1=weishu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890026}} (banned by Emperor [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Yang of Sui]]), with surviving fragments found in [[:en:Miyoshi Kiyotsura|Miyoshi Kiyoyuki]]’s “{{ill|Kakumei Kanbun|ja|革命勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|1=kakumei kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663819}}” (collected in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' vol. 26), it is written that following {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Yin Metal Rooster also embodies the metal element, and Xin represents yin energy, often associated with people becoming cold-hearted. Thus, a Yin Metal Rooster year is considered a year when the [[:en:Mandate of Heaven|Mandate of Heaven]] changes, marking revolutionary shifts in [[:en:Dynasty|dynasties]], known as the '''{{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|辛酉革命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛酉|1=Yin Metal Rooster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q904743}}'''.
In Japan, era name changes ({{ill|Era name|ja|改元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}}) during Yin Metal Rooster years were meant to prevent political upheaval. This practice started in Yin Metal Rooster of [[:en:901|901]] ([[:en:Shōtai|Shōtai]] 4), when scholar-official Miyoshi Kiyoyuki proposed changing the era name to '''[[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]'''. Frequently, era names were changed consecutively in both {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Yin Metal Rooster years, a custom that continued until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], though such a precedent does not exist in China.
{{Anchors|Xinyou Revolution|qq=}} During the Meiji era, scholar {{ill|Naka Michiyo|ja|那珂通世|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|1=Naka Michiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642793}} referenced ''Kakumei Kanbun'' with [[:en:Zheng Xuan|Zheng Xuan]]’s annotation: * “The way of Heaven is not distant; after every three and five, it returns. Six cycles ([[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) form one era, four sixes and two sixes overlap, and three sevens multiply to form 21 cycles, totaling 1,320 years.” Based on this, it was believed that every '''1,260 years''' (21 cycles × 60 years = 1,260, called ''Ichibu'') there would be a major revolution during a Yin Metal Rooster year. Applying this, Naka theorized that the 9th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]’s reign ([[:en:601|601]]) marked such a revolutionary year, suggesting that Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]]’s enthronement occurred 1,260 years earlier, in [[:en:660 BC|660 BC]].
Alternatively, some interpret the annotation as indicating a '''1,320-year cycle''' (60 × 22 = 1,320). In this case, the pivotal year would shift to the year of the [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]], three years after Yin Metal Rooster, considered the '''Jiazi Reform Year''' ({{ill|Jiazi Reform|ja|甲子革令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲子|1=Yang Wood Rat|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190890}}). This places the starting point at [[:en:664|664]] AD, the year after the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]], aligning with the legendary founding date of 660 BC.
==Months of Yin Metal Rooster== A '''Yin Metal Rooster month''' occurs in [[:en:August|August]] of years ending in 3 or 8 (corresponding to Heavenly Stems {{ill|Gui|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} and {{ill|Wu|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}). Depending on the calendar system, this could refer to the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} month or the solar term period between [[:en:Bailu (solar term)|White Dew]] and the day before [[:en:Hanlu|Cold Dew]].
A '''Yin Metal Rooster day''' is the 7th day in the {{ill|Hassen|ja|八専|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=}} sequence, excluding intercalary days.

<!-- == * Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: かのととり (kanoto-tori) ***Onyomi: しんかのとり (shinka-no-tori) ***Onyomi: しんゆう (shinyū) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): sinyu ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
--> ==Years of Yin Metal Rooster== {{Anchors|Xinyou Year|qq=}} A [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] year that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 60 is a '''Yin Metal Rooster year'''.
Yin Metal Rooster is considered to have revolutionary significance.
In the ancient Chinese texts known as {{ill|Wei Shu|ja|緯書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|1=weishu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890026}} (banned by Emperor [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Yang of Sui]]), with surviving fragments found in [[:en:Miyoshi Kiyotsura|Miyoshi Kiyoyuki]]’s “{{ill|Kakumei Kanbun|ja|革命勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|1=kakumei kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663819}}” (collected in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' vol. 26), it is written that following {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Yin Metal Rooster also embodies the metal element, and Xin represents yin energy, often associated with people becoming cold-hearted. Thus, a Yin Metal Rooster year is considered a year when the [[:en:Mandate of Heaven|Mandate of Heaven]] changes, marking revolutionary shifts in [[:en:Dynasty|dynasties]], known as the '''{{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|辛酉革命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛酉|1=Yin Metal Rooster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q904743}}'''.
In Japan, era name changes ({{ill|Era name|ja|改元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}}) during Yin Metal Rooster years were meant to prevent political upheaval. This practice started in Yin Metal Rooster of [[:en:901|901]] ([[:en:Shōtai|Shōtai]] 4), when scholar-official Miyoshi Kiyoyuki proposed changing the era name to '''[[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]'''. Frequently, era names were changed consecutively in both {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Yin Metal Rooster years, a custom that continued until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], though such a precedent does not exist in China.
{{Anchors|Xinyou Revolution|qq=}} During the Meiji era, scholar {{ill|Naka Michiyo|ja|那珂通世|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|1=Naka Michiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642793}} referenced ''Kakumei Kanbun'' with [[:en:Zheng Xuan|Zheng Xuan]]’s annotation: * “The way of Heaven is not distant; after every three and five, it returns. Six cycles ([[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) form one era, four sixes and two sixes overlap, and three sevens multiply to form 21 cycles, totaling 1,320 years.” Based on this, it was believed that every '''1,260 years''' (21 cycles × 60 years = 1,260, called ''Ichibu'') there would be a major revolution during a Yin Metal Rooster year. Applying this, Naka theorized that the 9th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]’s reign ([[:en:601|601]]) marked such a revolutionary year, suggesting that Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]]’s enthronement occurred 1,260 years earlier, in [[:en:660 BC|660 BC]].
Alternatively, some interpret the annotation as indicating a '''1,320-year cycle''' (60 × 22 = 1,320). In this case, the pivotal year would shift to the year of the [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]], three years after Yin Metal Rooster, considered the '''Jiazi Reform Year''' ({{ill|Jiazi Reform|ja|甲子革令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲子|1=Yang Wood Rat|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190890}}). This places the starting point at [[:en:664|664]] AD, the year after the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]], aligning with the legendary founding date of 660 BC.
==Months of Yin Metal Rooster== A '''Yin Metal Rooster month''' occurs in [[:en:August|August]] of years ending in 3 or 8 (corresponding to Heavenly Stems {{ill|Gui|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} and {{ill|Wu|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}). Depending on the calendar system, this could refer to the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} month or the solar term period between [[:en:Bailu (solar term)|White Dew]] and the day before [[:en:Hanlu|Cold Dew]].
A '''Yin Metal Rooster day''' is the 7th day in the {{ill|Hassen|ja|八専|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=}} sequence, excluding intercalary days.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q904743]] Wikidata name: Yin Metal Rooster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Chinese|l=Yin Metal Rooster|kanji=辛酉|romaji=kanoto tori|onyomi=shinyū|p=xīyǒu|w=hsin-yu|mi=ɕin⁵³ i̯ɤʊ̯²¹|y=san1 jau5|j=san1 jau5|ci=sɐn jɐu|mc=sin jɨu|oc-bs=sin jɯʔ|qq=}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yin Metal Rooster also known as Yin Metal Rooster''' ({{ill|1=Yin Metal Rooster|2=ja|辛酉|ja|1=Yin Metal Rooster|2=ja|辛酉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/辛酉|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1=Yin Metal Rooster|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Metal Rooster|qq=}}) is one of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
It is the 58th combination in the cycle, preceded by {{ill|Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|zh|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|ko|경신|fr|Singe de métal|de|Metall-Affe|en|lt_ko=경신|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and followed by {{ill|Yang Water Dog|ja|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|zh|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|ko|임술|fr|Chien de eau|de|Wasser-Hund|en|lt_ko=임술|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Heavenly Stem]] {{ill|Mizunoto (癸)|cdo|Gŭi (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Gui (tige céleste)|gan|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|hak|Kui (thiên-kôn)|ja|Mizunoto|ko|계 (천간)|vi|Quý (Thiên can)|wuu|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|za|Gveiq (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Kúi (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|zh|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} represents yin {{ill|metal|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal Branch]] [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster (雞)]] also represents yin metal, forming a {{ill|harmonious relationship|ja|比和|1=Wu Xing|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|1=比和|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|lt=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|qq=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|4=el|5=Ου Σιν|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/harmonious ==Pronunciation== * English: Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau5 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-tori ***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-tori ***Onyomi 1: shinyuu ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinyu (신유) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
<!-- == * Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: かのととり (kanoto-tori) ***Onyomi: しんかのとり (shinka-no-tori) ***Onyomi: しんゆう (shinyū) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): sinyu ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
--> ==Years of Yin Metal Rooster== {{Anchors|Xinyou Year|qq=}} A [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] year that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 60 is a '''Yin Metal Rooster year'''.
Yin Metal Rooster is considered to have revolutionary significance.
In the ancient Chinese texts known as {{ill|Wei Shu|ja|緯書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|1=weishu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890026}} (banned by Emperor [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Yang of Sui]]), with surviving fragments found in [[:en:Miyoshi Kiyotsura|Miyoshi Kiyoyuki]]’s “{{ill|Kakumei Kanbun|ja|革命勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|1=kakumei kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663819}}” (collected in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' vol. 26), it is written that following {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Yin Metal Rooster also embodies the metal element, and Xin represents yin energy, often associated with people becoming cold-hearted. Thus, a Yin Metal Rooster year is considered a year when the [[:en:Mandate of Heaven|Mandate of Heaven]] changes, marking revolutionary shifts in [[:en:Dynasty|dynasties]], known as the '''{{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|辛酉革命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛酉|1=Yin Metal Rooster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q904743}}'''.
In Japan, era name changes ({{ill|Era name|ja|改元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}}) during Yin Metal Rooster years were meant to prevent political upheaval. This practice started in Yin Metal Rooster of [[:en:901|901]] ([[:en:Shōtai|Shōtai]] 4), when scholar-official Miyoshi Kiyoyuki proposed changing the era name to '''[[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]'''. Frequently, era names were changed consecutively in both {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Yin Metal Rooster years, a custom that continued until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], though such a precedent does not exist in China.
{{Anchors|Xinyou Revolution|qq=}} During the Meiji era, scholar {{ill|Naka Michiyo|ja|那珂通世|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|1=Naka Michiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642793}} referenced ''Kakumei Kanbun'' with [[:en:Zheng Xuan|Zheng Xuan]]’s annotation: * “The way of Heaven is not distant; after every three and five, it returns. Six cycles ([[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) form one era, four sixes and two sixes overlap, and three sevens multiply to form 21 cycles, totaling 1,320 years.” Based on this, it was believed that every '''1,260 years''' (21 cycles × 60 years = 1,260, called ''Ichibu'') there would be a major revolution during a Yin Metal Rooster year. Applying this, Naka theorized that the 9th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]’s reign ([[:en:601|601]]) marked such a revolutionary year, suggesting that Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]]’s enthronement occurred 1,260 years earlier, in [[:en:660 BC|660 BC]].
Alternatively, some interpret the annotation as indicating a '''1,320-year cycle''' (60 × 22 = 1,320). In this case, the pivotal year would shift to the year of the [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]], three years after Yin Metal Rooster, considered the '''Jiazi Reform Year''' ({{ill|Jiazi Reform|ja|甲子革令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲子|1=Yang Wood Rat|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190890}}). This places the starting point at [[:en:664|664]] AD, the year after the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]], aligning with the legendary founding date of 660 BC.
==Months of Yin Metal Rooster== A '''Yin Metal Rooster month''' occurs in [[:en:August|August]] of years ending in 3 or 8 (corresponding to Heavenly Stems {{ill|Gui|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} and {{ill|Wu|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}). Depending on the calendar system, this could refer to the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} month or the solar term period between [[:en:Bailu (solar term)|White Dew]] and the day before [[:en:Hanlu|Cold Dew]].
A '''Yin Metal Rooster day''' is the 7th day in the {{ill|Hassen|ja|八専|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=}} sequence, excluding intercalary days.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q904743]] Wikidata name: Yin Metal Rooster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Chinese|l=Yin Metal Rooster|kanji=辛酉|romaji=kanoto tori|onyomi=shinyū|p=xīyǒu|w=hsin-yu|mi=ɕin⁵³ i̯ɤʊ̯²¹|y=san1 jau5|j=san1 jau5|ci=sɐn jɐu|mc=sin jɨu|oc-bs=sin jɯʔ|qq=}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yin Metal Rooster also known as Yin Metal Rooster''' ({{ill|1=Yin Metal Rooster|2=ja|辛酉|ja|1=Yin Metal Rooster|2=ja|辛酉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/辛酉|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1=Yin Metal Rooster|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Metal Rooster|qq=}}) is one of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
It is the 58th combination in the cycle, preceded by {{ill|Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|zh|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|ko|경신|fr|Singe de métal|de|Metall-Affe|en|lt_ko=경신|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and followed by {{ill|Yang Water Dog|ja|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|zh|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|ko|임술|fr|Chien de eau|de|Wasser-Hund|en|lt_ko=임술|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Heavenly Stem]] {{ill|Mizunoto (癸)|cdo|Gŭi (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Gui (tige céleste)|gan|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|hak|Kui (thiên-kôn)|ja|Mizunoto|ko|계 (천간)|vi|Quý (Thiên can)|wuu|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|za|Gveiq (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Kúi (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|zh|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} represents yin {{ill|metal|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/metal|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/metal|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster (雞)]] also represents yin metal, forming a {{ill|harmonious relationship|ja|比和|1=Wu Xing|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|1=比和|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|lt=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|qq=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|4=el|5=Ου Σιν|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/harmonious philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218652}}.
==Pronunciation== * English: Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau5 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-tori ***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-tori ***Onyomi 1: shinyuu ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinyu (신유) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
<!-- == * Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: かのととり (kanoto-tori) ***Onyomi: しんかのとり (shinka-no-tori) ***Onyomi: しんゆう (shinyū) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): sinyu ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
--> ==Years of Yin Metal Rooster== {{Anchors|Xinyou Year|qq=}} A [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] year that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 60 is a '''Yin Metal Rooster year'''.
Yin Metal Rooster is considered to have revolutionary significance.
In the ancient Chinese texts known as {{ill|Wei Shu|ja|緯書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|1=weishu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890026}} (banned by Emperor [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Yang of Sui]]), with surviving fragments found in [[:en:Miyoshi Kiyotsura|Miyoshi Kiyoyuki]]’s “{{ill|Kakumei Kanbun|ja|革命勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|1=kakumei kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663819}}” (collected in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' vol. 26), it is written that following {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Yin Metal Rooster also embodies the metal element, and Xin represents yin energy, often associated with people becoming cold-hearted. Thus, a Yin Metal Rooster year is considered a year when the [[:en:Mandate of Heaven|Mandate of Heaven]] changes, marking revolutionary shifts in [[:en:Dynasty|dynasties]], known as the '''{{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|辛酉革命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛酉|1=Yin Metal Rooster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q904743}}'''.
In Japan, era name changes ({{ill|Era name|ja|改元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}}) during Yin Metal Rooster years were meant to prevent political upheaval. This practice started in Yin Metal Rooster of [[:en:901|901]] ([[:en:Shōtai|Shōtai]] 4), when scholar-official Miyoshi Kiyoyuki proposed changing the era name to '''[[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]'''. Frequently, era names were changed consecutively in both {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Yin Metal Rooster years, a custom that continued until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], though such a precedent does not exist in China.
{{Anchors|Xinyou Revolution|qq=}} During the Meiji era, scholar {{ill|Naka Michiyo|ja|那珂通世|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|1=Naka Michiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642793}} referenced ''Kakumei Kanbun'' with [[:en:Zheng Xuan|Zheng Xuan]]’s annotation: * “The way of Heaven is not distant; after every three and five, it returns. Six cycles ([[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) form one era, four sixes and two sixes overlap, and three sevens multiply to form 21 cycles, totaling 1,320 years.” Based on this, it was believed that every '''1,260 years''' (21 cycles × 60 years = 1,260, called ''Ichibu'') there would be a major revolution during a Yin Metal Rooster year. Applying this, Naka theorized that the 9th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]’s reign ([[:en:601|601]]) marked such a revolutionary year, suggesting that Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]]’s enthronement occurred 1,260 years earlier, in [[:en:660 BC|660 BC]].
Alternatively, some interpret the annotation as indicating a '''1,320-year cycle''' (60 × 22 = 1,320). In this case, the pivotal year would shift to the year of the [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]], three years after Yin Metal Rooster, considered the '''Jiazi Reform Year''' ({{ill|Jiazi Reform|ja|甲子革令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲子|1=Yang Wood Rat|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190890}}). This places the starting point at [[:en:664|664]] AD, the year after the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]], aligning with the legendary founding date of 660 BC.
==Months of Yin Metal Rooster== A '''Yin Metal Rooster month''' occurs in [[:en:August|August]] of years ending in 3 or 8 (corresponding to Heavenly Stems {{ill|Gui|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} and {{ill|Wu|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}). Depending on the calendar system, this could refer to the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} month or the solar term period between [[:en:Bailu (solar term)|White Dew]] and the day before [[:en:Hanlu|Cold Dew]].
A '''Yin Metal Rooster day''' is the 7th day in the {{ill|Hassen|ja|八専|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=}} sequence, excluding intercalary days.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q904743]] Wikidata name: Yin Metal Rooster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Chinese|l=Yin Metal Rooster|kanji=辛酉|romaji=kanoto tori|onyomi=shinyū|p=xīyǒu|w=hsin-yu|mi=ɕin⁵³ i̯ɤʊ̯²¹|y=san1 jau5|j=san1 jau5|ci=sɐn jɐu|mc=sin jɨu|oc-bs=sin jɯʔ|qq=}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yin Metal Rooster also known as Yin Metal Rooster''' ({{ill|1=Yin Metal Rooster|2=ja|辛酉|ja|1=Yin Metal Rooster|2=ja|辛酉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/辛酉|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1=Yin Metal Rooster|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Metal Rooster|qq=}}) is one of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
It is the 58th combination in the cycle, preceded by {{ill|Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|zh|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|ko|경신|fr|Singe de métal|de|Metall-Affe|en|lt_ko=경신|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and followed by {{ill|Yang Water Dog|ja|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|zh|1=Yang Water Dog|2=ja|壬戌|ko|임술|fr|Chien de eau|de|Wasser-Hund|en|lt_ko=임술|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Water Dog|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Heavenly Stem]] {{ill|Mizunoto (癸)|cdo|Gŭi (tiĕng-găng)|fr|Gui (tige céleste)|gan|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|hak|Kui (thiên-kôn)|ja|Mizunoto|ko|계 (천간)|vi|Quý (Thiên can)|wuu|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|za|Gveiq (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Kúi (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|zh|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} represents yin {{ill|metal|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/metal|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/metal|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster (雞)]] also represents yin metal, forming a {{ill|harmonious relationship|ja|比和|1=Wu Xing|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|1=比和|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|2=ja|3=五行思想#比和（ひわ）|lt=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|qq=Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)|4=el|5=Ου Σιν|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/harmonious philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q218652}}.
==Pronunciation== * English: Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau5 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-tori ***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-tori ***Onyomi 1: shinyuu ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinyu (신유) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
<!-- == * Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu ** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: かのととり (kanoto-tori) ***Onyomi: しんかのとり (shinka-no-tori) ***Onyomi: しんゆう (shinyū) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): sinyu ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu
--> ==Years of Yin Metal Rooster== {{Anchors|Xinyou Year|qq=}} A [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] year that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 60 is a '''Yin Metal Rooster year'''.
Yin Metal Rooster is considered to have revolutionary significance.
In the ancient Chinese texts known as {{ill|Wei Shu|ja|緯書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Shu|qq=|1=weishu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24890026}} (banned by Emperor [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Yang of Sui]]), with surviving fragments found in [[:en:Miyoshi Kiyotsura|Miyoshi Kiyoyuki]]’s “{{ill|Kakumei Kanbun|ja|革命勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumei Kanbun|qq=|1=kakumei kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663819}}” (collected in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' vol. 26), it is written that following {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Yin Metal Rooster also embodies the metal element, and Xin represents yin energy, often associated with people becoming cold-hearted. Thus, a Yin Metal Rooster year is considered a year when the [[:en:Mandate of Heaven|Mandate of Heaven]] changes, marking revolutionary shifts in [[:en:Dynasty|dynasties]], known as the '''{{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|辛酉革命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛酉|1=Yin Metal Rooster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q904743}}'''.
In Japan, era name changes ({{ill|Era name|ja|改元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Era name|qq=|1=era change|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498853}}) during Yin Metal Rooster years were meant to prevent political upheaval. This practice started in Yin Metal Rooster of [[:en:901|901]] ([[:en:Shōtai|Shōtai]] 4), when scholar-official Miyoshi Kiyoyuki proposed changing the era name to '''[[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]]'''. Frequently, era names were changed consecutively in both {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and Yin Metal Rooster years, a custom that continued until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], though such a precedent does not exist in China.
{{Anchors|Xinyou Revolution|qq=}} During the Meiji era, scholar {{ill|Naka Michiyo|ja|那珂通世|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Michiyo|qq=|1=Naka Michiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642793}} referenced ''Kakumei Kanbun'' with [[:en:Zheng Xuan|Zheng Xuan]]’s annotation: * “The way of Heaven is not distant; after every three and five, it returns. Six cycles ([[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]]) form one era, four sixes and two sixes overlap, and three sevens multiply to form 21 cycles, totaling 1,320 years.” Based on this, it was believed that every '''1,260 years''' (21 cycles × 60 years = 1,260, called ''Ichibu'') there would be a major revolution during a Yin Metal Rooster year. Applying this, Naka theorized that the 9th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]’s reign ([[:en:601|601]]) marked such a revolutionary year, suggesting that Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]]’s enthronement occurred 1,260 years earlier, in [[:en:660 BC|660 BC]].
Alternatively, some interpret the annotation as indicating a '''1,320-year cycle''' (60 × 22 = 1,320). In this case, the pivotal year would shift to the year of the [[:en:Wood Rat|Yang Wood Rat]], three years after Yin Metal Rooster, considered the '''Jiazi Reform Year''' ({{ill|Jiazi Reform|ja|甲子革令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiazi Reform|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 甲子|1=Yang Wood Rat|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190890}}). This places the starting point at [[:en:664|664]] AD, the year after the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]], aligning with the legendary founding date of 660 BC.
==Months of Yin Metal Rooster== A '''Yin Metal Rooster month''' occurs in [[:en:August|August]] of years ending in 3 or 8 (corresponding to Heavenly Stems {{ill|Gui|ja|癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|lt=Mizunoto (癸)|2=ja|3=癸|4=zh|5=癸|1=Mizunoto (癸)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gui|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunoto (癸)|qq=}} and {{ill|Wu|ja|戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|lt=Tsuchinoe (戊)|2=ja|3=戊|4=zh|5=戊|1=Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wu|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe (戊)|qq=}}). Depending on the calendar system, this could refer to the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} month or the solar term period between [[:en:Bailu (solar term)|White Dew]] and the day before [[:en:Hanlu|Cold Dew]].
A '''Yin Metal Rooster day''' is the 7th day in the {{ill|Hassen|ja|八専|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassen|qq=}} sequence, excluding intercalary days.
